testcase.name: should create empty frame
should handle single roll [pins: 0, expectedPinsRemaining: 10, expectedScore: 0, #0]
should handle single roll [pins: 5, expectedPinsRemaining: 5, expectedScore: 5, #1]
should handle single roll [pins: 10, expectedPinsRemaining: 0, expectedScore: 10, #2]
should handle two rolls [roll1: 3, roll2: 4, isStrike: false, isSpare: false, isComplete: true, #0]
should handle two rolls [roll1: 5, roll2: 5, isStrike: false, isSpare: true, isComplete: true, #1]
should handle two rolls [roll1: 10, roll2: 0, isStrike: true, isSpare: false, isComplete: true, #2]
should handle two rolls [roll1: 0, roll2: 10, isStrike: false, isSpare: true, isComplete: true, #3]
should handle last frame with strike [roll1: 10, roll2: 5, roll3: 5, expectedScore: 20, #0]

failure.message: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Cannot knock down more pins than available

testcase.failure: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Cannot knock down more pins than available at com.example.Frame.addRoll(Frame.java:26) at com.example.FrameSpec.should handle last frame with strike(FrameSpec.groovy:70)

testcase.name: should handle last frame with strike [roll1: 10, roll2: 10, roll3: 10, expectedScore: 30, #1]

failure.message: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Cannot knock down more pins than available

testcase.failure: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Cannot knock down more pins than available at com.example.Frame.addRoll(Frame.java:26) at com.example.FrameSpec.should handle last frame with strike(FrameSpec.groovy:70)

testcase.name: should handle last frame with strike [roll1: 10, roll2: 0, roll3: 0, expectedScore: 10, #2]
should validate roll constraints - valid rolls [pins: 3, #0]
should validate roll constraints - valid rolls [pins: 5, #1]
should validate roll constraints - invalid rolls [pins: 6, #0]
should validate roll constraints - invalid rolls [pins: 10, #1]
should handle frame equality [roll1: 3, roll2: 4, isLastFrame: false, shouldEqual: true, #0]
should handle frame equality [roll1: 5, roll2: 5, isLastFrame: false, shouldEqual: true, #1]
should handle frame equality [roll1: 10, roll2: 0, isLastFrame: true, shouldEqual: true, #2]
should display frame correctly [roll1: 3, roll2: 4, expectedDisplay: [3, 4], #0]
should display frame correctly [roll1: 5, roll2: 5, expectedDisplay: [5, /], #1]
should display frame correctly [roll1: 10, roll2: -1, expectedDisplay: [X], #2]
should display frame correctly [roll1: 0, roll2: 10, expectedDisplay: [0, /], #3]

failure.message:
Condition not satisfied:
frame.toString() == expectedDisplay | | | | | [0, X] | [0, /] [0, X] false 1 difference (83% similarity) [0, (X)] [0, (/)]

testcase.failure:
Condition not satisfied:
frame.toString() == expectedDisplay | | | | | [0, X] | [0, /] [0, X] false 1 difference (83% similarity) [0, (X)] [0, (/)]
at com.example.FrameSpec.should display frame correctly(FrameSpec.groovy:141)
